system.$description: General-purpose system accent color. Both link and the focus ring point here. The previous value, iOS dark system blue #0A84FF, failed both consumers: as link text it measured 3.70:1 on the flat surface and 1.76:1 on the modal (AA needs 4.5:1), and as the focus ring it cleared 3:1 on only one of the four dark surfaces. Dark's four surfaces are the gray 600-900 rungs, so the same squeeze that calibrated border to 300 applies, but text is stricter: 100 is the darkest rung that clears 4.5:1 on every surface (5.24:1 on modal; 200 fails at 4.11). The hue stays blue; the rung is what the floor forces.

surface.$description: Raised surface (card/panel). Dark: tonal gray ladder.

shadow.$description: Elevation shadow color. Black, not gray.900 as in light: dark's own surfaces run from gray.900 up, so a gray.900 shadow lands on a surface it barely differs from — 1.30:1 against gray.800, and 1.45:1 against gray.600 even at half opacity. A shadow has to be darker than what it falls on.

shadow-umbra.$description: The near, hard shadow. Dark draws depth tonally and the shadow only separates what the tonal ladder cannot: the ladder tops out at gray.600, so modal and notification share a surface and a toast would otherwise vanish into the dialog under it. 0.3 black gives a 1.56:1 edge there — legible, and still slight next to light's lift (Art.1, elevation.md §3 'ごく僅か').

shadow-penumbra.$description: The far, soft shadow. Holds roughly light's umbra:penumbra ratio.

focus-ring.$description: Global focus ring color. Defaults to app.system; override per theme.

border.$description: The component's outline, for `outlined` (emphasis.md). 300, not an alias of `bg`. `bg` is 500 in dark so white text clears AA on it (color.md), and 500 sits at 2.51:1 against the flat surface, below WCAG 1.4.11's 3:1 for a component boundary, and worse on the lighter ones (1.93 / 1.45 / 1.19). Dark's four surfaces span #292F36-#526071; 300 is the darkest rung clearing 3:1 on all four (7.06 / 5.42 / 4.08 / 3.36). Unlike soft-bg (color.md §8), this one has a solution.

scrim.$description: Veil behind modal / dialog / drawer. Stacking order is --layer-scrim; this token carries the visual value only (elevation.md §6).
